meta_title: For Students: portfolio building to Solve can't debug code (Advanced Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to portfolio building and overcome can't debug code. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Portfolio Building to Solve Debugging Woes
content.seo_description: Master advanced debugging by building a portfolio. Use this expert-level checklist featuring Antigravity and Claude Code to level up your engineering career.
content.answer_block: Debugging isn't a lack of talent; it's a lack of process. By building a portfolio-grade project, you move from passive reading to active architectural problem-solving. Utilize Claude Code for iterative analysis and Antigravity for automated logic verification to turn your 'cannot debug' frustration into a repeatable engineering workflow.
content.intro:
The screen glows with a sea of red error logs, and your cursor blinks in rhythmic mockery. You’ve spent hours chasing a race condition that refuses to die, and the weight of 'imposter syndrome' begins to settle in. This is the crucible where most aspiring engineers quit, but it is also where the top 1% are forged.

True mastery isn't found in tutorials; it is earned in the trenches of your own architecture. By shifting your focus from 'fixing code' to 'building a portfolio-worthy system,' you stop being a student and start being an architect. This guide will show you how to leverage AI-assisted debugging to transform your technical debt into a high-value career asset.

steps.title: Architectural Scoping
steps.description: Define the boundaries of your project. A portfolio project must solve a real-world data problem to demonstrate your ability to handle complexity.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my project requirements. Identify potential bottlenecks and suggest an architectural pattern that minimizes state-management bugs.

steps.title: Automated Logic Verification
steps.description: Use Antigravity to verify your core logic before writing the UI. This prevents debugging cascading failures later.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a suite of edge-case tests for this function. Focus on null-pointer exceptions and asynchronous race conditions.

steps.title: Context-Aware Refactoring
steps.description: Feed your error logs directly into Claude Code to identify the root cause of systemic bugs rather than patching symptoms.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Based on this stack trace, identify the architectural flaw that caused the memory leak.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --ask 'Analyze the error log at ./logs/debug.log and propose a fix for the main.ts module'

steps.title: Documentation for Impact
steps.description: Document your debugging process. Employers value engineers who can articulate how they solved complex problems.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a technical post-mortem for my portfolio based on the bug I just fixed, highlighting the engineering trade-offs.

steps.title: Portfolio Deployment
steps.description: Deploy your project to a live environment. Real-world traffic is the ultimate debugger for your application.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review my deployment configuration for security vulnerabilities and performance bottlenecks before I ship.

pro_tips: Never fix a bug without writing a regression test; it turns a wasted hour into a permanent asset.
Use Claude Code to explain code you didn't write—it forces you to verify your mental model against reality.
Treat your logs as your primary data source; if you can't log it, you can't architect it.
Limit your scope to one complex feature rather than five simple ones; depth impresses recruiters more than breadth.
Always refactor code *after* the test passes; never optimize prematurely while the system is failing.
content.next_steps: Once your project is live, share the repository with a technical mentor for a code review, then start your second project using the same architectural framework.
content.conversion_block: Your career is built on the projects you finish, not the errors you dread. Stop debugging in circles and start building a portfolio that commands attention. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
